content:
Boqun Feng said:

(the PerCpu variable part is taken from a recent glance at what Mitchell Levy is working on)

@Mitchell Levy @Boqun Feng how is PerCpu coming along? Do you have patches I can try out?

There have been several versions in the list, latest: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250828-rust-percpu-v3-0-4dd92e1e7904@gmail.com/

Or do you mean something else?

Miguel Ojeda said:

There have been several versions in the list, latest: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250828-rust-percpu-v3-0-4dd92e1e7904@gmail.com/

Or do you mean something else?

It occurred to me to search my inbox after I wrote the message.
A v4 will probably be out soon, though it'll be substantially similar to v3. Biggest change is some logic to properly drop contents of dynamic pcpu variables
Happy to send a WIP of that if it's relevant to your usecase

Mitchell Levy said:

A v4 will probably be out soon, though it'll be substantially similar to v3. Biggest change is some logic to properly drop contents of dynamic pcpu variables

I think v3 is good for now, but I will keep an eye out for v4. Thanks!
